---
title: "Spelling and Grammar Check - {{Lesson Name}}"
author: brad_cannell
date: {{YYYY-MM-DD}}
date-modified: {{YYYY-MM-DD}}
category: lesson_development
tags:
  - r
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  eval: false
---

# Overview

- Conversation about editing the spelling and grammar in this lesson.

# Prerequisites
- **Git Pull** to make sure you have the most current version of the prompts.
- Install in the system kernel. The virtual environments cause too many problems with Box.
- `import sys`
- `print(sys.executable)` Path the Python interpreter that is actually running the code. 
- Terminal: `which pip` Pip that will be used to install Python packages
- Terminal: `/usr/local/bin/python3 -m pip install keyring openai anthropic jupyter -U chatlas shiny`

# Load Packages

```{python}
from chatlas import ChatAnthropic
from openai import OpenAI
from httpcore import stream
from datetime import date
from pathlib import Path
import chatlas as ctl
import anthropic
import keyring
import json
import os
```

# File Paths and Environment Variables

They are passed to the AI assistant below.

```{python}
# OpenAI Models: https://platform.openai.com/docs/models
# Anthropic Models: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/about-claude/models/overview
model = "gpt-5-nano-2025-08-07"

# Today's date
today = date.today().isoformat()

# Course and lesson name
# By default, these are injected from Convert Chapters.qmd
# They can also be altered manually.
course_name = "{{Course Name}}"
lesson_name = "{{Lesson Name}}"

# Lessone file and folder paths
# By default, these are injected from Convert Chapters.qmd
# They can also be altered manually.
lesson_file_path = Path("{{lesson_file_path}}")
lesson_folder_path = lesson_file_path.parent

# Lesson content
lesson_content = (lesson_file_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")

# Prompt paths
path_template_prompt_spelling_grammar = Path('/Users/bradcannell/Desktop/Git/AI/ewb-ai-assistant/prompts/Courses/Template Prompt - Spelling and Grammar Check.md')

# Tag SOP
path_template_tag_sop = Path('/Users/bradcannell/Library/CloudStorage/GoogleDrive-brad.cannell@epi-workbench.com/My Drive/SOPs/Course Author Wiki/02 Course Development/01 Categories and Tags/Categories and Tags.qmd')
tag_sop_content = path_template_tag_sop.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
```

```{python}
# Check that all paths exist
paths_to_check = {
    "lesson_file_path": lesson_file_path,
    "lesson_folder_path": lesson_folder_path,
    "path_template_prompt_spelling_grammar": path_template_prompt_spelling_grammar,
    "path_template_tag_sop": path_template_tag_sop
}

for name, path in paths_to_check.items():
    if not path.exists():
        raise FileNotFoundError(f"Path not found — {name}: {path}")
    print(f"✓  {name}: {path}")
```

# Load helper functions

Run in terminal:
- `/usr/local/bin/python3 -m pip install --editable "/Users/bradcannell/Desktop/Git/AI/my-ai-assistant/py_scripts"`
- Restart interpreter.

```{python}
# Load functions from py_scripts/py_scripts
from py_scripts import (
    prompt_extract_system_prompt,
    prompt_extract_user_message,
    prompt_fill_placeholders,
)
```

# Expose API

This assumes you've already created an API key on [openai's website](https://platform.openai.com/api-keys), and that you saved the API key in keyring using `keyring.set_password("API Name", "User", "API Key")`.

```{python}
# Retrieve the password from the system keyring
openai_api = keyring.get_password("ewb-course-development", "brad")

if openai_api is not None:
    # Set the retrieved value as a temporary environment variable
    os.environ['OPENAI_API_KEY'] = openai_api
    print("Environment variable 'OPENAI_API_KEY' set successfully within the script.")
else:
    print("Could not retrieve password from keyring.")
```

# Read the Template Prompt

```{python}
# Raw system (developer) section of the prompt template.
system_prompt = prompt_extract_system_prompt(path_template_prompt_spelling_grammar)

# Raw user message section of the prompt template.
user_message_raw = prompt_extract_user_message(path_template_prompt_spelling_grammar)

# Inject course and lesson name into user message
user_message = prompt_fill_placeholders(
    text = user_message_raw,
    replacements = {
        "lesson_name": lesson_name,
        "course_name": course_name,
        "lesson_content": lesson_content
    },
)
```

# Create Chat Object

```{python}
chat = ctl.ChatOpenAI(
    model = model,
    system_prompt = system_prompt
)
```

# Pass the Template Prompt to the AI Assistant

```{python}
chat.chat(user_message, echo='none')
```

## Response

```{python}
#| echo: false
print(chat)
```

# Write the revisions to a Markdown File for Review

- It can be tricky/annoying to copy and paste the changes from the console.
- Just delete the markdown file when you are done making revisions.

```{python}
(lesson_folder_path / "ai" / ("spelling_and_grammar_check_" + today + ".md")).write_text(
    str(chat.get_last_turn()),
    encoding='utf-8'
)
```

# Follow-up and Refinement

A section for optional follow-up questions and refinements. 
- Optionally, create a fork of the chat. `import copy; chat_fork = copy.deepcopy(chat)`
- Asks follow-up questions in interactive mode - `chat.app()`.

# 🏷️ Add Tags

- At this point, we should have a final version of the chapter. Go ahead and ask the AI Assistant to suggest some tags.

```{python}
tag_request = """
Return a bulleted list of tags for this lesson. 

- Return only the 5-10 most relevent tags.

    - In this context, relevent refers to the content learners are likely to search for, and the tags they are likely to use in their search.

- Do not use words from the lesson title in tags.

    - For example, don't return "Data Transfer" as a tag for a lesson titled, "Data Transfer in R".

- Do not add any formatting (e.g., bold) to the items in the list.

- Use this format when returning function tags: Function: function_name [Language]. For example, "Function: group_by [R]"

- When 3 or more functions from the same package are used in the lesson, tag the entire package.

    - Use this format when returning package tags: Package: package_name [Language]. For example, "Package: dplyr [R]"

- Below each tag, provide a tag description.

    - Use this template for function tag descriptions: "This course or lesson contains information about using the R [function_name] function. This tag is not intended for use when the course or lesson just happens to contain code that uses the R [function_name] function."

    - Use this template for package tag descriptions: "This course or lesson contains information about using the [package_name] R package. This tag is not intended for use when the course or lesson just happens to contain code that uses the [package_name] package."

I'm attaching an SOP about tags with further guidance.

## Tag request SOP:

"""
tag_request = tag_request + tag_sop_content
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
chat.chat(tag_request, echo='none')
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
print(chat)
```

# Save Chat History

Optional, but necessary to return to the conversation later.

```{python}
# Export chat history as a markdown file
# This version is easier to pass back to to the chat object in the future.
# chat_history_path_md = (lesson_folder_path / "ai" / ("lesson_spelling_grammar_" + today + ".md"))
# chat.export(chat_history_path_md, overwrite=True)

# Export chat history as an HTML file
# chat_history_path_html = (lesson_folder_path / "ai" / ("lesson_spelling_grammar_" + today + ".html"))
# chat.export(chat_history_path_html, overwrite=True)

# Export chat history as a json file
# Useful for saving/loading the history to/from a database or file.
# chat_history_path_json = (lesson_folder_path / "ai" / ("lesson_spelling_grammar_" + today + ".json"))
# with open(chat_history_path_json, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
#     json.dump(
#         [json.loads(x) for x in turns_json],
#         f,
#         indent=2,
#         ensure_ascii=False
#     )
```

## Condense

You can also optionally condense the history by asking the LLM to summarize it. This is useful if you want to keep the context of the conversation, but don’t want to provide the entire history to the model on each new submission.

```{python}
# chat.chat("Can you summarize our conversation so far?")
# chat.set_turns([chat.get_last_turn()])
```

## Load chat history

To optionally load the chat history later.

```{python}
# Import chat history as a md file
# chat_history_path_md = (lesson_folder_path / "ai" / ("lesson_revisions_history_" + "yyyy-mm-dd" + ".md"))
# history_md = chat_history_path_md.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

# Import chat history as a json file
# chat_history_path_json = (lesson_folder_path / "ai" / ("lesson_revisions_history_" + "yyyy-mm-dd" + ".json"))
# history_json = json.loads(chat_history_path_json.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
```

# Render to HTML

Render to HTML (Optional)
- Click the Preview button.
- Type `quarto render "path/Lesson Revisions.qmd"` in the terminal.
